description: Generate feature/flow documentation for a repo from scratch, FlowDoc-style (document what the code DOES, not a file inventory). Spawns: root epic -> a single discover spawner -> one doc bead per manifest item (created at runtime) -> a finalize bead gated on all the discovered docs. Replaces the flowdoc / flowdoc-user agent-loop. Template/section/naming fidelity is carried by the inlined _FlowDocGuide.md the discover step authors (there is no external 'flowdoc' skill). The maintainer edition documents code structure (Features/Flows/Endpoints/Views/Concepts); the user edition organizes docs by the Diataxis framework (Getting-Started/Guides/Tutorials/Reference/Concepts), follows task-oriented writing rules, and never exposes code, file paths, or internal URLs. Pour with --var audience=maintainer (default) or --var audience=user. Run via 'bd mol pour flowdoc-generate --var audience=...'.

audience.description: maintainer = developer docs in __docs/ (implementation detail, file refs); user = end-user docs in _docs/ (no code/paths, task-oriented voice).

steps.title: FlowDoc: generate {{audience}} documentation
steps.description:
Umbrella for a from-scratch FlowDoc documentation pass ({{audience}} edition).

AUDIENCE MAP (read this first, every child obeys it):
  - audience = maintainer -> write to __docs/ ; voice = for developers;
    include implementation detail, file paths, mermaid, API/View tables.
  - audience = user       -> write to _docs/  ; voice = for end users;
    NO source paths, NO code, NO internal URLs; task/outcome oriented.
    Organize docs by the Diataxis framework: Getting-Started / Guides /
    Tutorials / Reference / Concepts.

This epic has exactly two authored children: 'discover' (runs first and spawns
one bead per documentation item) and 'finalize' (runs last, gated on every
spawned doc). Do not work this epic directly — work its leaves.

steps.title: FlowDoc {{audience}}: discover & scaffold, then spawn one bead per doc
steps.description:
DISCOVERY + FAN-OUT (one bead). Your bead id is the value shown as
"Complete beads issue <id>" at the top of this goal — call it $SELF below.

AUDIENCE = {{audience}}.  DOCS_DIR = __docs/ if maintainer, _docs/ if user.

TEMPLATE CARRIER (GAP-0): there is NO external "flowdoc" skill. The SOLE
carrier of template/section/naming fidelity is DOCS_DIR/_FlowDocGuide.md, which
you author in Part A step 3 below. Every doc bead reads it. Author it verbatim
from the templates in this step so a generic agent reproduces output identical
in SHAPE (section order, table column headers, mermaid scaffolds, callout
markers, naming) to the FlowDoc reference agents. Do not paraphrase the
templates into a bare section list — carry the column layouts and skeletons.

== Part A: survey & scaffold (do all of this, then spawn, then stop) ==
1. SURVEY with discovery methodology (the HOW, not just the what):
   list_files(".", recursive=True); read dependency manifests, configs, entry
   points. Identify tech stack, features, flows, API endpoints, views/pages,
   cross-cutting concepts. Use these per-framework grep recipes to find them:
     * Endpoints: Express `app.(get|post|put|delete|patch)` / router.*;
       FastAPI `@app.`/`@router.(get|post|...)`; Flask `@app.route`;
       Spring `@(Get|Post|Put|Delete|Request)Mapping`;
       ASP.NET `[Http(Get|Post|...)]` / `MapGet`; Go `mux.HandleFunc`/`r.Get`.
     * Views/pages: React Router `<Route `/`createBrowserRouter`;
       Next.js `app/**/page.(t|j)sx` + `pages/**`; Vue `<router-view>`/routes
       array; Angular `RouterModule.forRoot`; SvelteKit `src/routes/**/+page`.
     * CLI commands: argparse `add_parser`/`add_argument`; click `@click.command`;
       cobra `&cobra.Command`; commander `.command(`.
     * Concepts: cross-cutting auth/caching/queue/state modules, shared
       middleware, base classes, config/env schema.
   BE<->FE LINKAGE (required): determine which views call which endpoints
   (grep fetch/axios/useQuery/HttpClient targets against the endpoint list) so
   View docs can cross-link their API deps and Feature docs span both tiers.
2. Write the top-level overview:
   - maintainer -> DOCS_DIR/Architecture.md with ALL of these sections, in order:
       (a) Quick Start for Maintainers (clone/run/test/where-to-look bullets)
       (b) Tech Stack — table | Layer | Choice | Why this choice |
       (c) System Diagram — a mermaid graph of the major components
       (d) Features at a Glance — table | Feature | What it does | Docs link |
       (e) Key Flows — list linking the most important Flow docs
       (f) External Dependencies — table | Dependency | Used for | Failure Impact |
       (g) Directory Guide — table | Path | Responsibility |
       (h) API Surface — table | Method | Path | Purpose | Doc | PLUS an
           "API Conventions" block (auth scheme, error envelope, versioning,
           pagination) shared across endpoints
       (i) Views & Pages — table | Route | View | Purpose | Doc | PLUS a
           "Navigation Structure" mermaid diagram and a "Shared Layouts" note
   - user -> first DETECT THE DELIVERY MODEL (G6): inspect the repo to
     decide how end users actually reach the product — installed locally
     (desktop/mobile/CLI install steps, packaging configs, app-store
     manifests) vs accessed via a hosted URL (web app, SPA, server-rendered
     site). This decision drives two things downstream:
       * Getting-Started shape: if installed-locally, the Getting-Started
         band INCLUDES an Installation page; if URL-accessed, OMIT
         Installation and lead with an "Accessing <Product>" page instead.
       * URLs: document ONLY the production URL a real user would visit;
         NEVER localhost, dev, or staging hosts.
     Then write DOCS_DIR/Overview.md with these sections: # <Product>,
     ## What Is It, ## Who Is It For, ## Key Features, ## Requirements,
     ## Getting Started (link to the Getting-Started band), ## Next Steps.
     NO file paths, NO code, NO internal URLs.
3. Write DOCS_DIR/_FlowDocGuide.md — the authoring contract every doc bead
   follows. It MUST contain, VERBATIM, these section templates (with the table
   column headers, mermaid scaffolds, and callout markers shown) so output is
   identical in shape to the FlowDoc agents:

   --- Feature (maintainer) ---
     # <Name>
     ## What It Does            (1-2 sentences, behavior-first)
     ## Why It Exists           (the problem it solves)
     ## How It Works
       ### User Perspective     (what the user sees/does)
       ### System Perspective   (what the code does end to end)
     ```mermaid
     sequenceDiagram            (the request/interaction sequence)
     ```
     ## Key Data Shapes         (DTO / request / response JSON skeletons in
                                 fenced ```json blocks — real field names)
     ## API Surface             (table | Method | Path | Purpose | -> Endpoint doc)
     ## Implementation Map      (table | Responsibility | File path | Symbol |)
     ## Configuration           (table | Key | Default | Effect |)
     ## Edge Cases              (> [!WARNING] one per edge case)
     ## Error Scenarios         (table | Trigger | Behavior | User sees |)
     ## Testing                 (how it's tested / how to test)
     ## Related                 (bidirectional links to flows/endpoints/views)

   --- Flow (maintainer) ---
     # <Name>
     ## What Happens / ## Trigger / ## Outcome
     ```mermaid
     flowchart TD               (the step graph)
     ```
     ## Step-by-Step            (table | # | What | Where (file:symbol) | Failure mode |)
     ## Data Transformations    (input -> output at each hop)
     ## Performance Characteristics  (latency/throughput/N+1 notes, sync vs async)
     ## Failure Handling        (retries, timeouts, compensation)
     ## Key Log Messages        (table | Log line | Where | Means |)
     ## Common Issues           (table | Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |)  (debugging)
     ## Related

   --- Endpoint (maintainer only) ---
     # <METHOD> <path>
     ## Overview                (table | Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |)
     ## Request
       ### Path/Query Params    (table | Name | In | Type | Required | Notes |)
       ### Headers              (table | Header | Required | Notes |)
       ### Body                 (fenced ```json request skeleton)
       ### Validation Rules     (table | Field | Rule | Error |)
       ### Rate Limit           (one row: limit / window / scope)
     ## Response
       ### Success              (status + fenced ```json response skeleton)
       ### Errors               (table | Status | Code | When |)
     ## Implementation Map      (table | Responsibility | File path | Symbol |)
     ```mermaid
     sequenceDiagram            (client -> handler -> store)
     ```
     ## Example                 (a real `curl` invocation with headers + body)
     ## Related

   --- View (maintainer only) ---
     # <Name> (<route>)
     ## Overview                (table | Route | Auth | Purpose |)
     ## URL Params              (table | Param | Type | Required | Notes |)
     ## What It Does / ## User Actions
     ```mermaid
     flowchart TD               (page structure / component tree)
     ```
     ## Components              (table | Component | Responsibility | File |)
     ## State Management        (table | State | Source | Updated by |)
     ## Data Flow               (a mermaid sequenceDiagram: view <-> API)
     ## API Dependencies        (table | Endpoint | Used for | -> Endpoint doc |)
     ## States                  (Loading / Empty / Error states described)
     ## Accessibility           (keyboard, ARIA, focus, contrast notes)
     ## Responsive Behavior     (breakpoint behavior)
     ## Related

   --- Concept (maintainer) ---
     # <Name>
     ## What Is It / ## Why This Approach
     ## How It Works            (+ a concrete example)
     ## Where Used              (links to features/flows that rely on it)
     ## Conventions             (> [!IMPORTANT] the rules to follow)
     ## Anti-Patterns           (> [!CAUTION] what not to do)
     ## Related

   ===================== USER EDITION (Diataxis) =====================
   For audience=user IGNORE the maintainer templates above (Feature/Flow/
   Endpoint/View/Concept) — they are maintainer-only. User docs are
   classified by the Diataxis framework into Getting-Started / Guides
   (how-to) / Tutorials / Reference / Concepts, each with the template
   below. Every user template obeys the LEAKAGE RULE inline (G7), it is NOT
   a finalize-only sweep: NO source paths, NO code or shell/install
   snippets, NO class/function names, NO localhost/dev/staging URLs —
   describe the UI the user sees ("the Receiving screen", "tap Submit"),
   never the implementation behind it.

   --- Getting-Started page (user) ---
     # Quick Start: <achieve a first concrete outcome>
     ## What You'll Achieve   (the end result in one line)
     ## Prerequisites         (account / access / device — user-facing only)
     ## Step 1..N: <action>   (numbered; each step = ONE user action plus
                               the expected result the user should see; use
                               Option A / Option B sub-steps for branches)
     ## Common Issues         (table | Symptom | What to do |)
     ## What You Learned / ## Next Steps  (links onward to Guides/Tutorials)
     (Installation page exists here ONLY if the delivery model is
      installed-locally; otherwise an "Accessing <Product>" page leads.)

   --- Guide / How-To (user) ---
     # How to <task>
     ## What You'll Learn / ## Prerequisites
     ## Overview              (1-2 sentences of context)
     ## Step 1..N: <sub-task> (numbered actions, each with its expected
                               result; nest 1..n bullet sub-actions/step)
     ## Troubleshooting       (table | Problem | Fix |)
     ## Related Guides        (bidirectional links)

   --- Tutorial (user) ---
     # Tutorial: <end-to-end scenario>
     ## What You'll Achieve / ## Before You Begin / ## The Scenario
     ## Step 1..N: <action>   (a narrative walkthrough showing expected
                               output/screens; cover Scenario A/B/C
                               success + error branches a user may hit)
     ## Final Result / ## What You Learned / ## Next Steps

   --- Reference (user) ---
     # <Subject> Reference
     ## Overview
     ## <grouped reference sections>  (screens, menus, buttons, fields,
                                       icons — as tables | Item | What it
                                       does |, e.g. screen-by-screen)
     ## Tips / ## See Also

   --- Concept (user) ---
     # <Concept in plain language>
     ## What Is It / ## Why It Matters
     ## How It Works          (plain language, analogy-friendly, no code)
     ## Related

   --- FAQ (user; the finalize step writes/refreshes this) ---
     # Frequently Asked Questions
     grouped by theme (Getting Started / Troubleshooting / Common Tasks),
     each entry a "### <question>" heading + a short answer.

   USER WRITING RULES (encode these 7 in the guide; doc beads enforce all):
     1. Second person ("you tap", "you'll see"), never "the user".
     2. Active voice; imperative steps.
     3. Show, don't tell — describe exactly what appears on screen.
     4. One idea per paragraph; keep paragraphs short.
     5. Use > [!TIP]/[!NOTE]/[!WARNING] callouts for tips and gotchas.
     6. After every step, state the expected result the user should see.
     7. Link generously and bidirectionally between related pages.

   Rules to encode in the guide: features-not-files; flows-not-functions;
   why-over-what; behavior-first framing; link every reference bidirectionally;
   mermaid for every flow/sequence; > [!NOTE]/[!WARNING]/[!IMPORTANT]/[!CAUTION]
   callouts for gotchas; READ-ONLY source; output only under DOCS_DIR.
4. Write DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md: progress counters (Total / Done / Remaining)
   + every item to document, grouped, with BANDED type-segmented IDs (not
   flat 001..NNN) so the type is readable from the number and new items slot
   in without renumbering. Group + band by audience:
     - maintainer: Features / Flows / Endpoints / Views / Concepts;
       Features 001+, Flows 010+, Endpoints 050+, Views 060/070+,
       Concepts 080+.
     - user (Diataxis): Getting-Started / Guides / Tutorials / Reference /
       Concepts; Getting-Started 001+, Guides 010+, Tutorials 040+,
       Reference 060/070/080+, Concepts 090+. Per the detected delivery
       model, include an Installation item under Getting-Started ONLY if
       the product is installed-locally.
   Each line: "- [ ] NNN | <Type>: <Name> -> [<Name>](<Section>/<PascalName>.md)".
   NAMING (N1): section DIRS and item FILE names are BOTH PascalCase
   (e.g. Features/OrderSetManagement.md), matching the FlowDoc reference; the
   manifest link target and the actual filename must agree exactly.
5. Write the index.md files: DOCS_DIR/index.md plus one per section dir.

== Part B: fan out — ONE bead per manifest item (the deadlock-safe contract) ==
For EACH unchecked item in _Manifest.md, create exactly one child bead:
  bd create --type=task --priority=2 --parent=$SELF \
    --title='FlowDoc {{audience}}: <Type>: <Name>' \
    --description='Write DOCS_DIR/<Section>/<PascalName>.md following the <Type>
       template in DOCS_DIR/_FlowDocGuide.md for audience={{audience}}.
       Read the relevant source fresh, fill EVERY template section and table
       (no placeholders, real column values, real JSON field names, real
       file:symbol impl-map rows), include the mermaid diagram(s) the template
       calls for, link related docs bidirectionally, then tick this item
       -> [x] in _Manifest.md and bump the counters. Filename is PascalCase.
       Acceptance: file exists at the PascalCase path, all template sections +
       tables filled (no placeholders), mermaid present, links valid, manifest
       updated.'
WIRING RULES — violating these creates deadlocks, so obey exactly:
  - Parent EVERY spawned bead to $SELF (so the finalize fan-out gate counts it).
  - Do NOT add any dependency from a doc bead to the finalize bead.
  - Do NOT add blocks edges between doc beads — they are independent/parallel.
  - Do NOT create your own finalize/rollup bead; one already exists and waits.
  - Spawn AT LEAST ONE child (a tiny repo still gets >=1 doc).
  - Do NOT close any bead — the LLM judges close. (bead-chain blocks bd close.)

Stop after scaffolding + spawning. Summarize what you wrote and how many doc
beads you spawned so the judges can verify.

steps.title: FlowDoc {{audience}}: finalize docs (troubleshooting, indexes, links)
steps.description:
FINALIZE the {{audience}} documentation set. You only become ready once
'discover' and EVERY doc bead it spawned are closed (fan-out gate).

DOCS_DIR = __docs/ if maintainer, _docs/ if user.
1. Confirm every item in DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md is checked [x]; fix counters
   (Total / Done / Remaining must reconcile).
2. Write/refresh DOCS_DIR/Troubleshooting.md (maintainer) or DOCS_DIR/FAQ.md
   (user) from gotchas surfaced while documenting.
3. Verify all intra-doc links resolve, naming is PascalCase end to end, and
   every section index lists its docs.
4. (user edition only) Grep the docs for developer leakage — source paths,
   shell/install commands, localhost/staging URLs, class/function names — and
   replace with user-facing equivalents.
READ-ONLY source. Output only under DOCS_DIR. Do NOT close any bead yourself.
